note: Public-source learning summary. This app is not affiliated with NVIDIA and does not run real NVIDIA NIM, NeMo, or Nemotron models.

sources.title: NVIDIA NIM Microservices
points: NIM packages optimized inference microservices for deployment on NVIDIA-accelerated infrastructure.
NIM emphasizes OpenAI-compatible APIs, container deployment, portability, throughput, latency, and enterprise support.

sources.title: NVIDIA NIM for Large Language Models API Reference
points: NIM LLM exposes OpenAI-compatible endpoints such as /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/responses, /v1/models, tokenize, and detokenize.
Recent documentation also references streaming, tool calling, response retrieval, cancellation, and Anthropic-compatible messages endpoints.

sources.title: NVIDIA NIM Model Profiles
points: Model profiles choose runtime engines and deployment criteria for a specific model and hardware combination.
Profiles may be optimized for hardware or generic, with backends such as TensorRT-LLM, vLLM, and SGLang.

sources.title: Supported Models for NVIDIA NIM for LLMs
points: Supported hardware examples include data-center GPUs and professional RTX-class GPUs with different memory limits.
The app models GPU memory as a practical planning constraint for mock deployment choices.

sources.title: NVIDIA NeMo
points: NeMo is described as a toolkit for managing the AI agent lifecycle, including data processing, fine-tuning, evaluation, safety, and observability.
The simulator treats NeMo as the lifecycle layer around models, data, evaluation, and guardrails.

sources.title: NVIDIA NeMo Framework Docs
points: NeMo Framework supports building, customizing, and deploying generative AI models, including LLMs and multimodal models.
The app includes mock fine-tuning and evaluation planning inspired by this workflow.

sources.title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails Docs
points: NeMo Guardrails adds programmable controls around LLM applications to block unsafe, off-topic, or malicious inputs and responses.
The simulator scores prompts for safety and flags common guardrail mistakes.

sources.title: NVIDIA NeMo Retriever
points: NeMo Retriever focuses on document ingestion, extraction, embedding, reranking, and RAG pipelines.
The simulator includes a mock retrieval path with embedding and reranking concepts.

concepts.name: NIM
concepts.plain: A packaged inference service for deploying optimized AI models behind familiar APIs.
concepts.watchFor: Do not confuse NIM with a model. NIM is the serving/deployment layer.

concepts.name: NeMo
concepts.plain: A broader toolkit for data, training, fine-tuning, evaluation, guardrails, and agent lifecycle work.
concepts.watchFor: NeMo is not just chat inference; it surrounds the lifecycle.

concepts.name: NeMo Guardrails
concepts.plain: Programmable controls that sit around an LLM app to steer or block behavior.
concepts.watchFor: Guardrails do not replace evaluation, access control, or secure tool design.

concepts.name: NeMo Retriever
concepts.plain: Retrieval pieces for RAG: ingestion, extraction, embeddings, reranking, and document-grounded context.
concepts.watchFor: RAG quality depends on data preparation and retrieval, not only the generator model.

concepts.name: Model profile
concepts.plain: A runtime/hardware choice that decides how a model should run efficiently.
concepts.watchFor: Selecting the biggest model without checking memory, latency, and throughput is a planning error.

mockModels.bestFor: Reasoning, enterprise assistant planning, RAG answer synthesis
Grounded enterprise answers using retrieved context
Choosing the most relevant passages after retrieval
